mnemonics: CSV(Can't Stand Vague) Paths: Always use the full, precise file path to avoid errors.
READR(Read-Awesome-Data-Rapidly): The readr package is your fast and friendly tool for importing rectangular data.
SAVE(Securely Archive Valuable Exports): Use saveRDS/readRDS for secure, efficient R object storage.
keyPoints: Use the readr package (e.g., read_csv()) for faster and more user-friendly importing of rectangular data like CSV files compared to base R functions.
The readxl package is essential for importing data from Excel files (.xlsx); use read_excel() and specify the sheet argument if needed.
Use saveRDS() and readRDS() to efficiently save and reload single R objects, preserving their structure and data types perfectly.
commonMistakes: Using a relative file path instead of an absolute one, causing the 'cannot open file' error when the working directory changes.
Forgetting to install and load the necessary package (e.g., readr, readxl, haven) before trying to use its import functions.
Assuming column data types were imported correctly; always check with str() or spec() and use the col_types argument to explicitly define them if needed.
